description_long: This object is a knife with a distinctive, elongated blade and a handle featuring a large circular hole near the top and a smaller hole closer to the blade. The blade is straight and tapers to a point, with a series of ridges or grooves along its length, suggesting a design for piercing or drilling. The handle is relatively flat and rectangular, with a slight curve at the top and a cutout near the blade, possibly for ergonomic grip or attachment. The overall silhouette is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features. The object appears to be constructed primarily from metal, likely steel, with a matte or semi-gloss finish.
description: A utilitarian knife with a straight, pointed blade featuring ridges and a flat handle with two holes and a curved top. The design is functional and unadorned, likely made of metal.
description_view_0: Side view showing the flat handle with two holes and the full length of the ridged, pointed blade.
description_view_1: Edge-on view displaying the thin profile of the blade and handle, emphasizing the straightness and pointed tip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the same handle and blade features visible.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again highlighting the thinness and pointed blade.
attribute_blade_type: straight, pointed, single-edged with ridged or grooved profile
attribute_handle_design: flat, rectangular, with two circular holes and a slight curve at the top, likely metal
